response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, mobile, and some embedded background, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, hardware-software co-design, and deep-learning workload performance analysis — none of which appear in the resume. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and DLang low-latency work do not substitute for principal-level hardware architecture experience.
strengths: Long and varied software engineering career with low-latency systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Some C/C++ experience from National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing frameworks, workshops)
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Educational/experience profile is full-stack web/product engineering, not hardware/performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture expertise
No AI accelerator or DNN training/inference optimization experience
